summary: Request for SkiaSharp PDF output to preserve 1bpp (monochrome) image color depth instead of upsampling to 24bpp, which causes a ~10× file size increase in scanned-document workflows.

stepsToReproduce: Take a scanned PDF containing 1bpp images (~4 MB).
Modify the XPS file while keeping 1bpp images.
Use SkiaSharp to re-encode the XPS to a new PDF.
Observe the output PDF is 40+ MB because images are stored as 24bpp.
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: SkiaSharp PDF backend; XPS→PDF workflow on Windows

repoLinks.description: Related: PDF output size request for general PDF compression options

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: SKDocumentPdfMetadata still has no color-depth controls; the issue is architectural rather than version-specific.

analysis.summary: SkiaSharp's PDF pipeline always rasterizes through its full-color canvas (BGRA8888 or similar), losing the original 1bpp depth. No API currently lets callers request monochrome or grayscale-only PDF image encoding. Skia's PDF backend does have internal paths for encoding lower-depth images (e.g., CCITTFax for 1bpp), but none are surfaced in the C API or SkiaSharp metadata struct. Implementing this would require Skia-level changes plus a new SKDocumentPdfMetadata field.
analysis.rationale: This is a feature request because the current behavior is by design: Skia's canvas model normalizes all pixel data through its color pipeline, discarding the source bit depth. No existing SkiaSharp or Skia API allows round-tripping 1bpp images through PDF. The issue is valid and has community interest (+3 reactions, original author follow-up in 2025), but requires upstream Skia changes to expose CCITTFax/monochrome image embedding to SkiaSharp.

codeInvestigation.finding: All CreatePdf() overloads ultimately pass only SKDocumentPdfMetadata to sk_document_create_pdf_from_stream_with_metadata. No color-depth or pixel-format parameter exists on this code path.

SKDocumentPdfMetadata exposes Title, Author, Subject, Keywords, Creator, Producer, Creation, Modified, RasterDpi, PdfA, and EncodingQuality. There is no color-type, bit-depth, or grayscale option. Adding a new field (e.g., PreferGrayscale or ImageColorDepth) would be the correct extension point.

SKColorType includes Gray8 (8bpp grayscale) but no 1bpp monochrome type. Skia has no kMono_SkColorType; true 1bpp output would require embedding pre-encoded CCITTFax image data rather than going through the pixel pipeline.

SKDocumentPdfMetadataInternal mirrors the C sk_document_pdf_metadata_t struct: fTitle, fAuthor, fSubject, fKeywords, fCreator, fProducer, fCreation, fModified, fRasterDPI, fPDFA, fEncodingQuality. No color-depth field exists at the native boundary.

keySignals.text: the resulting pdf file now has a file size of 40+mb because all the 1bpp images are automatically converted to 24bpp by SkiaSharp
keySignals.interpretation: Core of the request: SkiaSharp's PDF rasterizer normalizes bit depth to 24bpp, inflating scanned-document PDFs by ~10×.

keySignals.text: input (PDF) --> printed (XPS) --> changes (XPS) --> save with SkiaSharp (PDF) / 1 bpp --> 1 bpp --> 1 bpp --> 24 bpp
keySignals.interpretation: The full pipeline preserves 1bpp everywhere except the final SkiaSharp PDF step, confirming the issue is in SKDocument/PDF encoding.

keySignals.text: Any updates on this? As PDF files made with SkiaSharp really grow a lot this way.
keySignals.interpretation: Original reporter bumped after ~2 years; still unresolved and actively impacting them.

workarounds: Use SKColorType.Gray8 when drawing the canvas surface to reduce from 24bpp to 8bpp. This cuts image data to 1/3 the size but not to 1bpp.
Lower EncodingQuality in SKDocumentPdfMetadata (e.g., 50-75) to apply JPEG compression to images. This reduces file size at the cost of sharpness — not acceptable for scanned documents per reporter.
Use an alternative PDF library (e.g., PDFSharp, iText7, or Windows WIC/Direct2D PDF APIs) that supports CCITT/G4 1bpp image encoding natively for the final PDF save step.
nextQuestions: Does Skia's C++ SkPDF::MakeDocument support a metadata flag or image-encoding hint for monochrome/CCITTFax encoding?
Is there a way to embed a pre-encoded CCITT image as an opaque PDF image via SkiaSharp without going through the canvas pixel pipeline?
Would a new SKDocumentPdfMetadata.Grayscale bool (similar to how SkPaint uses SKHighContrastConfig) be sufficient to request grayscale PDF output?

resolution.hypothesis: The SkiaSharp PDF backend would need to expose a color-depth hint (e.g., grayscale/monochrome mode) in SKDocumentPdfMetadata, backed by a new field in the Skia C API, that tells the PDF image encoder to use lower-bit encodings (e.g., CCITTFax G4 for 1bpp).

proposals.title: Workaround: draw using Gray8 surface
proposals.description: Create the PDF canvas surface with SKColorType.Gray8 and SKAlphaType.Opaque. This halves depth from 24bpp to 8bpp/grayscale, reducing file size by ~3×. Won't achieve 1bpp but is an immediate improvement for monochrome scans.

proposals.title: Feature: add color-depth/grayscale option to SKDocumentPdfMetadata
proposals.description: Add a new field (e.g., bool Grayscale or enum ImageDepth) to SKDocumentPdfMetadata and the underlying sk_document_pdf_metadata_t C struct. The Skia PDF backend would use this hint to select a lower-depth image encoding path (Gray8 or CCITTFax). Requires changes to the native C API shim and Skia's PDF backend.

proposals.title: Alternative: use a dedicated PDF library for the save step
proposals.description: Replace the SkiaSharp PDF save step with a library that natively supports CCITT/G4 compression (PDFSharp 6.x, iText7, or Windows WIC PDF). This avoids the color-depth limitation entirely for the final save step.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Alternative: use a dedicated PDF library for the save step
resolution.recommendedReason: Highest confidence and immediately actionable. SkiaSharp's canvas model is not designed for round-trip PDF image manipulation; a purpose-built PDF library is the correct tool for lossless 1bpp PDF encoding.

actionability.reason: Valid feature request with community interest (+3 reactions, author follow-up). The request requires upstream Skia C API changes plus SkiaSharp wrapper updates; keeping open to track the enhancement.

actions.description: Apply type/feature-request, area/SkiaSharp, backend/PDF, tenet/performance labels
Cross-reference issue #848 (PDF output size, general compression)
Acknowledge request, explain root cause, provide workarounds
